claim_supported: Analyzes the thermal-management/cooling-vs-power-density trade-off across AFPM topologies (single-stator double-rotor achieves best torque density but is hardest to cool; single-stator single-rotor and double-stator machines cool more easily but have lower power density) -- directly relevant to why aerospace AFPM adopters (e.g., Evolito) emphasize dielectric liquid cooling.
locator: IEEE Transactions on Transportation Electrification, DOI 10.1109/TTE.2023.3242698 (2023)
notes: Not independently fetched (IEEE Xplore paywall). DOI confirmed via search cross-reference including ADS abstract listing.

title: System-Level Optimization in Switched Reluctance Machine Design -- Current Trends, Methodologies, and Future Directions
authors_or_org: Unconfirmed authors (Applied Sciences, MDPI)
claim_supported: Reviews system-level design optimization (SLDOM) of switched reluctance motors (SRMs) -- rare-earth-magnet-free machines -- where machine and controller are co-optimized, reporting significant improvements in mitigating torque ripple and radial-force issues, the two classic SRM drawbacks versus permanent-magnet motors.
locator: Applied Sciences (MDPI), vol 15, issue 11, article 6275, published June 3, 2025
notes: Not independently fetched (MDPI 403 pattern seen elsewhere this session, though MDPI is normally more accessible; not attempted directly here). DOI confirmed via search cross-reference.

title: Modelling and design optimization of switched reluctance motors based on machine learning methods: Review
authors_or_org: Unconfirmed authors (Franklin Open, ScienceDirect)
claim_supported: Reviews machine-learning-based modelling and design-optimization techniques for switched reluctance motors, covering optimization of stator/rotor pole arc, stack length, turns-per-pole, and phase current -- evidence that SRM design remains an active computational-optimization research area, not a settled commodity.
locator: Franklin Open (Elsevier), DOI 10.1016/j.fraope.2025.100474 (2025)
notes: Note this ScienceDirect article listing (unlike the abs/pii pattern seen elsewhere) appeared open-access (Franklin Open is a gold-OA Elsevier title); still not independently fetched this session for full-text verification. DOI confirmed via search cross-reference.

title: High-Speed Kinetic Energy Storage System Development and ANSYS Analysis of Hybrid Multi-Layered Rotor Structure
authors_or_org: Unconfirmed authors (Applied Sciences, MDPI)
claim_supported: Compares solid steel, solid titanium, solid carbon-composite, and hybrid multi-layered (carbon-composite-wrapped-metal) flywheel rotor structures via finite-element (ANSYS) analysis, addressing the cost/weight/high-speed-strength trade-off central to flywheel energy-storage-system rotor design.
locator: Applied Sciences (MDPI), vol 15, issue 10, article 5759, published May 21, 2025
notes: Not independently fetched this session. DOI inferred from standard MDPI Applied Sciences numbering convention (10.3390/app + volume-padded-issue-article number) matching the URL structure; verifier should confirm via doi.org resolution.

title: Magnetocaloric Refrigerator/Freezer (DOE-funded, Oak Ridge National Laboratory / General Electric)
authors_or_org: Oak Ridge National Laboratory; General Electric; U.S. Department of Energy
claim_supported: DOE-funded (USD 2,105,000; August 2013-September 2016) cooperative project between Oak Ridge National Laboratory and General Electric developed a magnetocaloric-effect residential refrigerator/freezer eliminating high-global-warming-potential refrigerants, targeting 20% energy-consumption reduction versus DOE minimum-efficiency standards and projected 0.28-0.42 quad annual savings at full market penetration.
locator: energy.gov CMEI (Commercially Available Manufacturing Energy Initiative) project article, sections on funding, timeline, and performance targets
notes: Fetched directly this session; funding figure, timeline, and performance targets confirmed from the official DOE project page. Project concluded in 2016 -- older than the lane's typical window, but included as the concrete named-buyer-adjacent (GE as industrial partner) government R&D precedent for magnetocaloric refrigeration commercialization; a verifier should check for a more recent (2024-2026) DOE follow-on program.

title: Astemo rare-earth-free synchronous reluctance motor system for BEVs
authors_or_org: Hitachi Astemo, Ltd.
claim_supported: Announced October 27, 2025: a rare-earth-free synchronous reluctance motor system combining a 180 kW magnet-assisted (ferrite-magnet) main drive motor with a 135 kW pure (magnet-free) synchronous reluctance auxiliary motor (315 kW combined), using a multi-layer flux structure and oil-immersion cooling; targeted for practical vehicle application 'around 2030' and positioned as a first-in-world mass-production candidate replacing conventional permanent-magnet BEV drive motors. Exhibited at Japan Mobility Show 2025 (October 30, 2025). No customer/OEM named.
locator: Astemo official news release, astemo.com/en/news/20251027-01/
notes: Fetched directly this session; all performance figures and dates confirmed from Astemo's own press release.

authors_or_org: Piller Power Systems (Langley Holdings); Bergen Engines
claim_supported: Piller Power Systems announced (November 26, 2025) that its SHIELDX Dynamic Power Stabilization units -- which use mechanical inertia (flywheel-based) combined with bi-directional active power modules -- were selected to stabilize a 400 MW off-grid AI-data-center power plant (36 x 11.2 MW Bergen gas engines) on the US East Coast, delivered by E-Finity Distributed Generation as EPC; first engine shipment scheduled December 2025.
